evals.prompt: Create a README.md file in /tmp/test-auto-format-1/ for a small Node.js library called "date-utils". Include a title, description, installation instructions, a usage example with a code block, and a license section.
evals.expected_output: A README.md file is created and a formatter is run on it (Just recipe or npx fallback)
expectations: The agent created a README.md file in /tmp/test-auto-format-1/
The agent ran a formatter on the file (either $JUST_CMD format-md or npx --yes prettier@3 --write)
The agent attempted $JUST_CMD format-md first before falling back to npx
The agent detected the platform (uname) to choose sjust or ajust

evals.prompt: Edit the file /tmp/test-auto-format-2/CHANGELOG.md to add a new entry under today's date for a feature called "dark mode toggle". The file already exists with some content.
evals.expected_output: The CHANGELOG.md is edited and a formatter is run on it afterward (Just recipe or npx fallback)
expectations: The agent edited /tmp/test-auto-format-2/CHANGELOG.md with a dark mode toggle entry
The agent ran a formatter AFTER editing the file (either $JUST_CMD format-md or npx --yes prettier@3 --write)
The agent did NOT attempt to manually format the Markdown content
The agent detected the platform (uname) to choose sjust or ajust

evals.prompt: Create two files in /tmp/test-auto-format-3/: a CONTRIBUTING.md with guidelines for pull requests and code style, and an API.md documenting three REST endpoints (GET /users, POST /users, DELETE /users/:id). Include request/response examples.
evals.expected_output: Both .md files are created and a formatter is run on both
expectations: The agent created CONTRIBUTING.md in /tmp/test-auto-format-3/
The agent created API.md in /tmp/test-auto-format-3/
The agent ran a formatter on both files (either $JUST_CMD format-md or npx --yes prettier@3 --write, in one call or two)
The agent attempted $JUST_CMD format-md first before falling back to npx

evals.prompt: I just finished building a microservices app in /tmp/test-auto-format-4/ with three services: auth, billing, and notifications. Can you document the high-level architecture? Include how the services communicate and what tech stack each one uses.
evals.expected_output: A Markdown documentation file is created and a formatter is run on it, even though the user never explicitly asked for Markdown
expectations: The agent created a .md file (any name) in /tmp/test-auto-format-4/
The agent ran a formatter on the created file (either $JUST_CMD format-md or npx --yes prettier@3 --write)
The agent detected the platform (uname) to choose sjust or ajust
The agent did NOT attempt to manually reformat the Markdown content

evals.prompt: Create a short INSTALL.md file in /tmp/test-auto-format-5/ with instructions for installing a Python CLI tool called "logparse" (pip install, basic usage, requirements).
evals.expected_output: INSTALL.md is created. The agent tries the Just recipe first, it fails, and the agent falls back to npx prettier to format the file.
expectations: The agent created INSTALL.md in /tmp/test-auto-format-5/
The agent attempted $JUST_CMD format-md first (preferred path)
After $JUST_CMD format-md failed, the agent fell back to npx --yes prettier@3 --write
The npx prettier fallback ran successfully on the file
The agent did NOT stop or warn without trying the npx fallback first

evals.prompt: I have a file at /tmp/test-auto-format-6/NOTES.md. Can you check if it's properly formatted without changing it?
evals.expected_output: The agent runs the check variant (format-md-check or npx prettier --check) and reports the result without modifying the file
expectations: The agent ran a check command (either $JUST_CMD format-md-check or npx --yes prettier@3 --check)
The agent did NOT use --write or modify the file
The agent reported the formatting status to the user
The agent detected the platform (uname) to choose sjust or ajust
